title: How to watch the Parade of Sail by boat
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sail Boston 250's Parade of Sail occurs July 11 with over 60 tall ships from 20 countries entering Boston Harbor.
Charter operators and cruise companies offer on-water experiences for Parade of Sail, with prices ranging from $49 to $2,395.
Classic Sail Boston offers a private charter aboard Captiva for the Parade of Sail, pricing the entire excursion at $2,395.

title: Officials identify cyclist, 15, killed in Franklin crash as Bishop Feehan student
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Oliver Lewis, 15, a Bishop Feehan High School student, died after being hit by a vehicle in Franklin.
The incident occurred near King Street and the I-495 north off-ramp in Franklin; the driver is cooperating with police.
Counseling services are available to Bishop Feehan students following Lewis's death; a funeral service is yet to be announced.

title: NBA Insider on Jaylen Brown: ‘He’s going to get traded’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: NBA insider Brian Windhorst expects the Boston Celtics to trade Jaylen Brown after missing out on Giannis Antetokounmpo.
Celtics President Brad Stevens did not confirm Jaylen Brown's future with the team, remaining noncommittal about trade possibilities.
Jaylen Brown, 29, had a standout season with MVP recognition and is under contract with the Celtics through 2029.

title: Review & setlists: David Lee Roth and Sammy Hagar both hit Greater Boston. Who rocked harder?
author: Christopher Hurley
bullets: David Lee Roth performed at House of Blues, Boston on May 21 with a two-hour, 18-song set.
Sammy Hagar played the Xfinity Center, Mansfield on June 24, delivering a two-hour, 19-song performance.
Hagar's concert featured Joe Satriani and Michael Anthony, while Roth's show focused on Van Halen classics.

title: How did it get this bad for the Red Sox, and where should they go from here?
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: The Boston Red Sox currently hold the fewest wins in MLB, with a record of 32-46 this season.
Pitching and hitting struggles have contributed to the team's shortcomings, despite a strong performance from pitcher Sonny Gray.
Injuries, lack of developed young players, and poor roster depth have plagued the Red Sox throughout the season.

title: Boston has one of the best free attractions in America, according to USA Today readers
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Boston's Freedom Trail ranked No. 7 among USA Today's best free attractions in America.
The Freedom Trail spans 2.5 miles, featuring 16 historical sites from Boston Common to Bunker Hill.
USA Today's 10Best Readers’ Choice Awards named Saint Louis Zoo as the top free attraction in America.

title: ‘Until next time Boston’: Scottish newspaper ad thanks city for welcoming Tartan Army
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: The Tartan Army published a full-page ad in The Herald thanking Boston for its hospitality during Scotland's World Cup campaign.
Scottish fans made a positive impression in Boston, attending Red Sox games and donating to local charities.
Boston Mayor Michelle Wu signed a letter to establish Glasgow as a 'sister city' following the Tartan Army's visit.

title: A 3-bedroom 1800s Colonial in Marblehead — can you guess the price?
author: Emily Turner
bullets: The 3-bedroom Colonial in Marblehead was built in 1842 and spans 2,136 square feet.
The property features 2 full bathrooms, one half-bath, and has public sewer and water.
The 2026 property taxes are $11,359, and the listing price options are $1,895,000, $1,795,000, or $1,695,000.

title: After a tense process, City Council accepts Wu’s amended budget. Here’s what to know.
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston City Council accepted Mayor Michelle Wu's amended $4.9 billion budget ending a contentious budget season.
The council's amendment package restored $11.8 million in funding including $1.8 million for rental vouchers and $750,000 for youth jobs.
The budget process highlighted tensions between Wu and councilors, with protests and arrests marking the council's budget debates.

title: Metal manufacturer to build third Massachusetts plant, bringing over 1,000 jobs
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: VulcanForms to build a one-million-square-foot plant in Devens, adding over 1,000 jobs to the local economy.
The expansion is supported by a $21.26 million state tax credit and a $220 million Series D funding.
VulcanForms produces parts using metal 3D printers for aerospace, defense, healthcare, and consumer product industries.
